source_section.title_label_raw: Motor Vehicle Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements

sections.title_label_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: All accessory parking lots and parking spaces shall be located on the same lot as the main use.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: A change of use in an existing structure shall not require any additional parking, and parking that is already provided shall remain.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: Subsections 431(4) and 431(5) do not apply to parking spaces or accessory parking lots associated with any:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: low-density dwelling use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: cluster housing use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: Repealed (RC- May23/24;E-Jun 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: backyard suite use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: small shared housing use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: mobile home use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: short-term bedroom rental (RC-Feb 21/23;E-Sep 1/23) use; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: home occupation use.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: All accessory parking lots shall be required to be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: surfaced with a hard material such as asphalt, concrete, or permeable pavers; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: delineated by concrete curbs, with all motor vehicle parking spaces and driving aisles clearly delineated.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking General Motor Vehicle Parking Requirements
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 454(6), the parking or storage of motor vehicles is prohibited in any driveway or driving aisle associated with an accessory parking lot. Parking Space and Driving Aisle Dimensions

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Section 440, for any accessory parking lot, parking

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 440, for any accessory parking lot, parking
provisions.text: Subject to Section 440, for any accessory parking lot, parking structure, or parking internal to a building:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 440, for any accessory parking lot, parking
provisions.text: subject to Clause 432(1)(b), any required motor vehicle parking space shall be a minimum of 2.4 metres in width and 5.5 metres in length (Diagram 24); and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 440, for any accessory parking lot, parking
provisions.text: in the case of parking parallel to an internal driveway, any required motor vehicle parking space shall be a minimum of 6.1 metres in length (Diagram 24). Diagram 24: Motor vehicle parking space and driving aisle dimensions, per Section 432

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 440, for any accessory parking lot, parking
provisions.text: Subject to Section 440, driving aisles between rows of motor vehicle parking spaces in an accessory parking lot, in a parking structure, or for parking internal to a building shall be required to be 6.0 metres in width for two-way traffic, and 3.0 metres in width for one-way traffic (Diagram 24). Minimum or Maximum Number of Motor Vehicle Parking Spaces

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 433(5), Table 15 sets out the minimum

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 433(5), Table 15 sets out the minimum
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 433(5), Table 15 sets out the minimum number of motor vehicle parking spaces required or the maximum number of motor vehicle parking spaces permitted by zone for each listed use. Where a use is not listed in Table 15, no minimum or maximum parking requirement applies.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 433(5), Table 15 sets out the minimum
provisions.text: If there is more than one use requiring motor vehicle parking, as listed in Table 15, then the amount of motor vehicle parking required on-site shall be the cumulative total of what is required for each use.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 433(5), Table 15 sets out the minimum
provisions.text: Where bicycle parking spaces are required in Section 449, two additional bicycle parking spaces of any type may be provided in substitution for one required motor vehicle parking space, to a maximum of 25% of required motor vehicle parking spaces.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 433(5), Table 15 sets out the minimum
provisions.text: In addition to the substitution permitted in Subsection 433(3), one enhanced bicycle parking space may be substituted for a maximum of one required motor vehicle parking space.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 433(5), Table 15 sets out the minimum
provisions.text: No motor vehicle parking spaces are required for any use on a registered heritage property or in a building located in a heritage conservation district. Table 15: Required minimum or maximum number of motor vehicle parking spaces per lot, by zone and use Design Requirements DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, CDD-2, CDD-1 COR HR-2, HR-1 ER-3, ER-2, ER-1 CH2, CH1 CLI, LI, HRI INS, UC-2, UC-1 PCF, RPK Any other residential use not listed within this table Not required Not required Not required Not required Not required Not applicab le Not required Not applicab le Home office use Not require d Not required Not required Not required Not required Not required Not required Not required Home occupation use Maximu m 1 space Maximu m 1 space Maximu m 1 space Maximu m 1 space Maximu m 1 space Not applicabl e Not applicabl e Not applicabl e Work-live unit use Not required Not required Maxim um 2 space s for each work- live unit Not applicabl e Not applicabl e Not applicabl e Not applicabl e Not applicabl e Restaurant use; sections.title_label_raw: Where the calculation for motor vehicle parking results in a fraction of

provisions.heading_context_raw: Where the calculation for motor vehicle parking results in a fraction of
provisions.text: Where the calculation for motor vehicle parking results in a fraction of a motor vehicle parking space, the required number of motor vehicle parking spaces shall be rounded down to the nearest whole number. Parking Within a Front or Flanking Yard

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Section 436, for a low-density dwelling use

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 436, for a low-density dwelling use
provisions.text: Subject to Section 436, for a low-density dwelling use excluding a townhouse dwelling use in the ER-3 zone, a maximum of 30% of the lot may be used for the parking and maneuvering of motor vehicles, of which a maximum of 40% of the front or flanking yard may be used for the parking and maneuvering of motor vehicles.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 436, for a low-density dwelling use
provisions.text: In the ER-3 zone, for a townhouse dwelling use or multi-unit dwelling use, parking in a front yard is not permitted.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 436, for a low-density dwelling use
provisions.text: If a low-density dwelling use in the ER-3 zone is changed to a townhouse use or multi-unit dwelling use, an additional parking space in a front yard is not permitted. (RC- May23/24;E-Jun 13/24) Additional Parking Requirements within Young Avenue Sub-Area A (YA-A)

sections.title_label_raw: For a two-unit dwelling use, a three-unit dwelling use, a four-unit

provisions.heading_context_raw: For a two-unit dwelling use, a three-unit dwelling use, a four-unit
provisions.text: For a two-unit dwelling use, a three-unit dwelling use, a four-unit dwelling use, or a multi-unit dwelling use that contains up to 5 units within the Young Avenue Sub-Area A (YA-A), as shown on Schedule 3C, the following additional motor vehicle parking requirements shall apply to any exterior parking: (RC- May23/24;E-Jun 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: For a two-unit dwelling use, a three-unit dwelling use, a four-unit
provisions.text: be located within a rear yard; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: For a two-unit dwelling use, a three-unit dwelling use, a four-unit
provisions.text: not exceed 5 spaces. Commercial Vehicles

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 437(2), any commercial vehicle that

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 437(2), any commercial vehicle that
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 437(2), any commercial vehicle that exceeds a weight of 3,000 kilograms of gross vehicle weight, shall be prohibited in any ER-3, ER-2, ER- 1, CH-2, or CH-1 zone.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 437(2), any commercial vehicle that
provisions.text: A commercial vehicle described in Subsection 437(1) is permitted to park in any ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, or CH-1 zone, if:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 437(2), any commercial vehicle that
provisions.text: the vehicle is parked on the lot during the period of construction; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 437(2), any commercial vehicle that
provisions.text: the vehicle is necessary for the purposes of construction. Motor Vehicle Sharing Spaces

sections.title_label_raw: Any required motor vehicle parking space may be used as a motor

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any required motor vehicle parking space may be used as a motor
provisions.text: Any required motor vehicle parking space may be used as a motor vehicle sharing space. Parking Structures

sections.title_label_raw: All parking spaces associated with a parking structure use shall

provisions.heading_context_raw: All parking spaces associated with a parking structure use shall
provisions.text: All parking spaces associated with a parking structure use shall be required to be located inside the parking structure or on the roof of the parking structure.

provisions.heading_context_raw: All parking spaces associated with a parking structure use shall
provisions.text: Rooftop parking on a parking structure shall not be visible from any streetline.

provisions.heading_context_raw: All parking spaces associated with a parking structure use shall
provisions.text: Where any portion of a parking structure roof is not used for parking, it shall be landscaped if required in Sections 418 or 419.

provisions.heading_context_raw: All parking spaces associated with a parking structure use shall
provisions.text: All parking structures shall meet the requirements of Part V. Automated Vehicle Parking System

sections.title_label_raw: Motor vehicle parking space and driving aisle dimension

provisions.heading_context_raw: Motor vehicle parking space and driving aisle dimension
provisions.text: Motor vehicle parking space and driving aisle dimension requirements do not apply where an automated vehicle parking system is used.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Motor vehicle parking space and driving aisle dimension
provisions.text: An automated vehicle parking system shall be required to be located internal to a building or in a parking structure. Parking Lots

sections.title_label_raw: Parking lots are prohibited in all zones, except for accessory parking

provisions.heading_context_raw: Parking lots are prohibited in all zones, except for accessory parking
provisions.text: Parking lots are prohibited in all zones, except for accessory parking lots which meet the requirements of Sections 442, 443, or 444. Accessory Parking Lots: DD, CEN-2, CEN-1, and COR Zones

sections.title_label_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any DD, CEN-2, CEN-1,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any DD, CEN-2, CEN-1,
provisions.text: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any DD, CEN-2, CEN-1, or COR zone.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any DD, CEN-2, CEN-1,
provisions.text: The required number of parking spaces within accessory parking lots in any DD, CEN-2, CEN-1, or COR zone shall not exceed:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any DD, CEN-2, CEN-1,
provisions.text: for a property containing a transportation facility use: unlimited;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any DD, CEN-2, CEN-1,
provisions.text: for a property containing a grocery store use: 80; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any DD, CEN-2, CEN-1,
provisions.text: for any other property:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any DD, CEN-2, CEN-1,
provisions.text: 200 square metres in lot area or less: 5,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any DD, CEN-2, CEN-1,
provisions.text: greater than 200 square metres in lot area and less than 6,300 square metres in lot area: 5 plus one additional space for every 350 square metres of lot area, or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any DD, CEN-2, CEN-1,
provisions.text: 6,300 square metres in lot area or more: 20.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any DD, CEN-2, CEN-1,
provisions.text: Accessory parking lots in any DD, CEN-2, or CEN-1 zone shall not be located within any front or flanking yard, and shall not be located within the first 33% of the lot depth or lot width abutting any streetline (Diagram 25), unless located in an internal courtyard.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any DD, CEN-2, CEN-1,
provisions.text: Accessory parking lots in any COR zone shall not be located within a front yard and shall not be located within the first 33% of the lot depth abutting a front lot line, unless located in an internal courtyard. Diagram 25: Permitted location for an accessory parking lot in any DD, CEN-2, or CEN-1 zone, per Subsection 442(3) Accessory Parking Lots: DH Zone

sections.title_label_raw: Except for areas shown on Schedule 49, accessory parking lots

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except for areas shown on Schedule 49, accessory parking lots
provisions.text: Except for areas shown on Schedule 49, accessory parking lots are permitted in the DH zone.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except for areas shown on Schedule 49, accessory parking lots
provisions.text: Accessory parking lots in the DH zone shall not exceed 20 parking spaces.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except for areas shown on Schedule 49, accessory parking lots
provisions.text: Accessory parking lots in the DH zone shall not be located within any front or flanking yard, and shall not be located within the first 50% of the lot depth or lot width abutting any streetline, unless located in an internal courtyard. Accessory Parking Lots: All Other Zones

sections.title_label_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any HR-2, HR-1, INS,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any HR-2, HR-1, INS, or CDD-2 zone, but only in the side yards, rear yards, and internal courtyards.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: Accessory parking lots are permitted in a CH-2 or CH-1 zone and shall meet the following requirements:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: be located within any yard;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: not exceed groupings of 5 motor vehicle parking spaces; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: each grouping of motor vehicle parking spaces shall be separated by soft landscaping.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any UC-1, DND, H, PCF, or RPK zone, but shall not be located in any minimum required front or flanking setback, as specified on Schedule 18.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: Subject to Clauses 249(1)(a), 250(1)(a), 301(1)(a), and 302(1)(a), accessory parking lots are permitted in any CLI, LI, or HRI zone, within any yard excluding any required setback distances that apply to any main building.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: Accessory parking lots in a UC-2 zone shall not be located within the lesser of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: the first 33% of the lot depth or lot width abutting any streetline (Diagram 25); or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory parking lots are permitted in any HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: 30.0 metres from a streetline. Pedestrian Walks Through Accessory Parking Lots

sections.title_label_raw: A minimum of one pedestrian walk through an accessory

provisions.heading_context_raw: A minimum of one pedestrian walk through an accessory
provisions.text: A minimum of one pedestrian walk through an accessory parking lot shall be required, and shall provide a direct route between parking areas, building entrances, and the nearest streetline.

provisions.heading_context_raw: A minimum of one pedestrian walk through an accessory
provisions.text: Pedestrian walks within accessory parking lots shall be required to be no less than 1.8 metres wide.

provisions.heading_context_raw: A minimum of one pedestrian walk through an accessory
provisions.text: Pedestrian walks within accessory parking lots shall be delineated by an accessible raised surface, that is no less than 0.15 metre in height, and consisting of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: A minimum of one pedestrian walk through an accessory
provisions.text: poured concrete;

provisions.heading_context_raw: A minimum of one pedestrian walk through an accessory
provisions.text: brick pavers;

provisions.heading_context_raw: A minimum of one pedestrian walk through an accessory
provisions.text: stone pavers; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: A minimum of one pedestrian walk through an accessory
provisions.text: concrete pavers.

provisions.heading_context_raw: A minimum of one pedestrian walk through an accessory
provisions.text: Where a pedestrian walk crosses a driving aisle in an accessory parking lot, the surface of the pedestrian walk shall be delineated with a change of colour or material from the driving aisle. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)
